title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud has made Python User Defined Functions (UDFs) generally available in BigQuery, allowing developers to run custom Python code natively within SQL statements without infrastructure management. The feature supports standard library imports like BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers for serverless execution.
writeup: This video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which let developers run custom Python code natively inside SQL statements without managing infrastructure. It matters because data no longer needs to be extracted to a separate environment for Python processing: the code executes serverlessly within BigQuery. The takeaways are that Python UDFs support standard library imports such as BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers, and that custom logic can run alongside SQL through a managed Python runtime.
